memoryArtifacts.headline: History of William, Count of Holland (1167-1222)
memoryArtifacts.description:
William I (c. 1167 – 4 February 1222), Count of Holland from 1203 to 1222. He was the younger son of Floris III and Ada of Huntingdon.

Life

William was born in The Hague, but raised in Scotland. He …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Willem I van Holland於1167年4月2日出生在荷蘭霍蘭丹 哈格時，他的父親Floris III van Holland26歲，母親Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland27歲。他於1197年與Adelheid von Geldern在荷蘭福利曼德斯塔福倫結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1222年2月4日在荷蘭霍蘭利斯包格享年54歲，埋葬在荷蘭-拉藍德利斯包格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Willem I van Holland于1167年4月2日出生在荷兰霍蘭丹 哈格时，他的父亲Floris III van Holland26岁，母亲Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland27岁。他于1197年与Adelheid von Geldern在荷兰福利曼德斯塔福倫结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1222年2月4日在荷兰霍蘭利斯包格享年54岁，埋葬在荷兰-拉藍德利斯包格。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Willem I van Holland was born on 2 April 1167, in The Hague, Holland, Netherlands, his father, Floris III van Holland, was 26 and his mother, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, was 27. He married Adelheid von Geldern in 1197, in Staveren, Friesland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 4 February 1222, in Rijnsburg, Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 54, and was buried in Rijnsburg, South Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Willem I van Holland est né le 2 avril 1167, à La Haye, Holland, Pays-Bas, son père, Floris III van Holland, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Adelheid von Geldern en 1197, à Staveren, Frise, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 4 février 1222, à Rijnsburg, Holland, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Rijnsburg, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Willem I van Holland am 2. April 1167 in Den Haag, Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Floris III van Holland 26 und seine Mutter Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland 27. Er hat 1197 in Staveren, Friesland, Niederlande Adelheid von Geldern geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 4. Februar 1222 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Rijnsburg, Holland, Niederlande und wurde in Rijnsburg, Südholland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Willem I van Holland è nato il il 2 aprile 1167 nel Den Haag, Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Floris III van Holland, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Adelheid von Geldern nel 1197, nel Staveren, Friesland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 4 febbraio 1222, nel Rijnsburg, Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Willem I van Hollandが1167年4月2日にオランダホーランドデン ハアグで生まれた時，父Floris III van Hollandは26歳，母Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Hollandは27歳であった。1197年にオランダフリースランドスタバーンでAdelheid von Geldernと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1222年2月4日にオランダホーランドリジンスブルグで54歳で死去し，オランダズイド-hオランドリジンスブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Willem I van Holland(이)가 1167년 4월 2일 네덜란드 홀랜드 든 하아그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Floris III van Holland(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1197년 네덜란드 프리슬랜드 스타버에서 Adelheid von Geldern(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1222년 2월 4일 네덜란드 홀랜드 리제이스버그에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 리제이스버그에 묻혔다.

mn.2 April 1167–4 February 1222: 1167 оны Дөрөвдүгээр сарын 2–1222 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 4

pt.2 April 1167–4 February 1222: 2 de abril de 1167–4 de fevereiro de 1222
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Willem I van Holland nasceu em 2 de abril de 1167, em Den Haag, Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Floris III van Holland, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Adelheid von Geldern em 1197, em Staveren, Frísia, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de fevereiro de 1222, em Rijnsburg, Holland, Países Baixos, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willem I van Holland родился 2 апреля 1167 г. в местности ден хааг, холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Floris III van Holland было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Adelheid von Geldern в 1197 г. в местности ставирен, фриесланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 4 февраля 1222 г. в местности риджнсбург, холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности риджнсбург, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.2 April 1167–4 February 1222: 2 de abril de 1167–4 de febrero de 1222
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Willem I van Holland nació el 2 de abril de 1167, en Den Haag, Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Floris III van Holland, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Adelheid von Geldern en 1197, en Staveren, Frisia, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 4 de febrero de 1222, en Rijnsburg, Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos.
